original_findings: The lungs are well inflated and clear. The cardiomediastinal silhouette, hilar contours, and pleural surfaces are normal. There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

findings.sentence: The lungs are well inflated and clear.
findings.source_span: The lungs are well inflated and clear.
The cardiomediastinal silhouette, hilar contours, and pleural surfaces are normal.

original_findings: The lungs appear clear with the exception of small left and possibly trace right pleural effusion and accompanying atelectasis. Heart size is mildly enlarged with normal cardiomediastinal contours.

findings.sentence: Small left pleural effusion is present.
findings.source_span: small left and possibly trace right pleural effusion

original_findings: Small to moderate left pleural effusion has recurred, despite persistent left pleural drainage catheter. No pneumothorax. Persistent left lower lobe collapse. Right lung cardiomediastinal silhouette clear. Large but unchanged. Ventricular diversion device grossly unchanged in position. Midline and left pleural drains, Swan-Ganz catheter, left PIC line all in standard placements. Tip of the nasogastric tube lies above the upper margin of the clavicles, no less than 55 mm from the carina. No pneumothorax.

findings.sentence: Small to moderate left pleural effusion has recurred.
